desc:
This tall and narrow passage is dug out of hard, rich dirt. Aside from
occasional pangs of claustrophobia, you manage to walk fairly easily through
here. The air is full of the rich smell of fresh loam, mixed in with the bitter
odor of troll excrement. Up ahead to the north, the passage becomes more of
stone, with more dirt to the south.
name: Dirt Tunnel

desc:
Bending from the south to the west, the tunnel is completely made of stone. The
walls, floor, and ceiling appear to have been crudely mined from the application
of more strength than skill or tools. The only things you know of with the will
and strength to do this are the trolls that inhabit the lands around here. A
foul smell of troll sweat hangs in the air and makes your eyes water. Time to
move on.
name: Stone Tunnel

desc:
Moving along this narrow and tall passage, you brush up against roots and other
plant parts protruding from the hard-packed dirt. Fears about a cave in are not
that high, as the roots themselves hold all the dirt in place. Obviously, trolls
dug this passage out, and have been using it frequently. The acrid smell of
troll fills your nose and makes your eyes water.
name: Earthy Tunnel

desc:
Sloping slightly downwards, the tunnel makes its way further and further along
from south to north. Strange sounds echo hollowly through here, mostly muffled
by the earthy walls. You brush aside a large root with annoyance as you
contemplate getting out of here as quickly as possible, before the stench of
troll forces you to vomit.
name: Earthy Tunnel

desc:
Onwards, this irregular tunnel moves along under the massive trunks above. It
bends from the west to the north, seemingly ignoring any roots that might have
been growing here. The marks of crude cuts line some of the bigger roots,
testimony to the strength and purpose of the makers. Splayed footprints line the
dirt floor, with the occasional bone or excrement as well. You hurry along to
escape this foul place.
name: Earthy Tunnel

desc:
The trail leads east-west here. Big rocks have fallen down from the cliff. The
rocks in the middle of the trail were pushed aside so that horses and
horse-drawn carts can pass here.
name: Trail under the Cliffs

desc:
The trail leads east-west here. Under some rocks you discover a hidden camp-
fire. Considering the look of the ashes the fire is maybe two days old. You also
find hidden rubbish and the remains of a meal. Finding tracks of people who used
the campfire is very hard and you can only guess where they went.
name: Trail under the Cliffs

desc:
The trail leads east-west here. Broken off branches from trees and bushes are
lying on the ground. You wonder where they came from since you can't see any
trees or bushes here. Maybe they have fallen down the cliff and above you is a
forest or another kind of vegetation? Unluckily you can't see any green leaves
and your hope of finding living beings vanishes again.
name: Trail under the Cliffs

desc:
The trail leads east-west here. In the cliff to the north you can see the
entrance to a larger cave. Big footprints lead to the cave. The footprints look
like they were made by the paws of a gigantic bear.
name: Trail under the Cliffs

desc:
The trail leads east-west here. You can see strange paintings in small caves in
the cliff. One of the upper caves contains a painting of a dragon and in the
northern part of the cave you see a broken altar.
name: Trail under the Cliffs

desc:
A path is leading north up to a cliff. The trail leads east-west here and leads
further east under a high cliff. You find signs of tracks here. The tracks lead
from the east to the path north. The trails is used here at least sometimes.
name: Junction in the Trail

desc:
The trail leads east and west. East is a crossing of trails, while to the north
and south lie just barren, endless plains only interrupted by sky- high cliffs.
To the west the trail leads through a small canyon between two cliffs. The
canyon is blocked by the remains of a rockslide.
name: Trail

desc:
The path is leading north-south and fast up around a cliff. The path is very
narrow here and it looks like it is very seldom used. To the east you can see
overhanging rocks above your head, but none of them has fallen down yet!
name: Path near the Cliffs

desc:
The path bends south-east here. The rocks above you look more unstable and you
notice small ones on the ground. A weathered signpost is here, but the writing
has long ago faded away. You get a first glimpse of the landscape ahead. It
looks plain and empty.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The path is gentle here passing a cliff to the south. To the north are endless
empty plains. Rocks are falling down from the cliff and trying to avoid them you
nearly fall over the ones in the path. The cliff isn't so high here anymore and
you can climb up to its top. The path bends east-west here.
name: Rocky Path

desc:
The path leads west-east here. A dense fog is coming from the north what makes
it impossible to even guess what comes next and where you really are.
name: Rocky and Foggy Path

desc:
The path leads east-west. A dense fog is coming from the north what makes it
impossible to even guess what comes next and where you really are. What evil may
be hidden behind the fog, or is it maybe a hidden treasure? The path has reached
the same altitude.
name: Rocky and Foggy Path

desc:
The path leads east-west here and leads gently around a forest to the north.
Footprints are leading to the forest in the north. Oops, they really look big!
You see bones being thrown out of holes in the forest. Broken armour, weapons
and other equipment lies around and you are for sure not the first careless
traveller who has ended his life in a big cooking pot!
name: Gentle Path

desc:
The path leads east-west here and passes a forest to the north. Dark shadows lie
over the path and a cold wind from the north blows icy air into your face. Human
skeletons are pinned to stone stakes in front of the forest. It looks like evil
creatures have eaten the poor ones alive!
name: Gentle Path

desc:
The plain stretches in every direction. Stones cover the ground and from time to
time you find bushes and small areas where grass and other vegetation grows. It
looks like this barren landscape will go on forever. But wait! You find
something like a path where the rocks were pushed aside, but you can't discover
any tracks.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
You have entered an area of plain, barren plains. Spare vegetation grows here
and the ground is covered with frost. In the north you can see a high mountain-
range. The plain stretches from the east to the west as far as you can see.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
The progress here is very difficult. Large rocks are in your way and you have
problems to discover paths between them. On the other hand the rocks also give
you some shelter against the icy wind.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
You find tracks leading north here. The tracks are less hidden as you go
northwards. You can't escape the icy wind that comes down the mountains in the
north and you have just made it half the way to the mountains.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
You can barely see your own hands. The fog here is really unbelievably thick. It
just cannot be natural fog! The plain is barren and empty here and you feel that
there are no living beings near.
name: Foggy Plain

desc:
Fog, fog, fog! You feel a soft wind coming from the north; maybe that's the
direction where it comes from? The fog stops where the forest begins in the
east. Small round stones can be found and the ground is covered with frost.
name: Foggy Plain

desc:
The forest suddenly becomes very dark here. The trees build a solid top that
blocks the sunlight totally. You notice broken off branches and small openings
in the undergrowth. It looks like huge creatures walk through this forest. As
you progress deeper into the forest you see smoke coming out of holes in the
ground.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The forest is extremely dark here and the undergrowth is very thick. The trees
are overgrown with moss and it even hangs down from their branches. Gigantic
funguses grow on rotten tree stumps and thorny bushes are everywhere. Ouch! A
hole is in the ground.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The aren't so many rocks here as before and you feel the icy wind from the north
again. In front of a rock you make a cruel discovery. Two frozen mummies inside
of iron cages. Someone must have put them here that they froze to death.
name: Rocky Plain

desc:
The tracks slowly lead west and you discover hidden signs on the rocks. It is so
cold here that even the vegetation is completely frozen. Here it is where the
icy winds from the north hit the plain.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
Finally you have reached the mountains. The mountains go vertically up for quite
a while and build a cliff. Under the cliff you find shelter from the icy wind.
Slowly recovering you look around and are surprised. The vegetation that grows
here is dense and looks like someone has planted it. The tracks lead to the
west.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
Shadows lie over this plain and giving those not accustom to the lower light an
uneasy feeling. In the sky one can often seen black crows circling above. The
crow's eyes seem to pierce even the darkest shadows as if they are spying upon
the land looking for intruders.
name: Shady Plain

desc:
You find areas which look like fields, even when you are not sure what plants
grow here. The plants must be extremely resistant against frost. To the west is
the entrance to a small village.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
You are standing in front of a wooden gate that blocks the entrance to the
hillmen village. The village is surrounded by a wooden stockade. A small tower
is built above the gate for the guards on watch. Evil signs are painted on the
gate. It is pretty obvious that you will be hanged on the highest post
available.
name: Entrance to the Hillmen Village

desc:
You are under the watch-tower. The tunnel you are in is leading north inside the
village. Embrasures are in the wooden tunnel walls and you see a second, open
gate at the exit inside the village. Wagon tracks are in the mud and
horse-droppings cover the ground.
name: Inside the Gate

desc:
The town hall is the large wooden building that mainly consists of a single
room. A long wooden table stretches from the south to the north end of the room.
The table is surrounded by wooden chairs with odd looking patterns on them. At
the northern end of the table stands a large throne-like chair. The floor is
built completely out of wooden planks and the windows even have panes of glass.
The town hall is used for sittings of the village council or for feasts when the
weather is too cold outside, which it is most of the year of course.
name: Town Hall

desc:
The house's furniture is plain and simple. You see a wardrobe, two chairs and a
table. The floor consists out of stamped loam. Above you is the sleeping chamber
and a small storage room, but there is no obvious way up. In the south part of
the house is a fireplace which is also used as a hearth. The shutters are closed
and can be made waterproof with oily fabrics.
name: Hillmen House

desc:
A huge stone-portal can be seen to the west. This must be the entrance to the
grave of an Angmar Lord. Pictures of evil spirits are painted on the portal and
you see a sign that tells you that this is the grave of an Angmar war-leader.
You wonder again why this grave isn't guarded more. You can look through holes,
in the portal and the walls, into the grave but you don't see any evil spirits
or skeletons in the grave.
name: Before a Portal

desc:
A stone made sarcophagus is in the west part of the room. It is simple and
without carvings on its sides, just a smooth sarcophagus. There are no treasure
chests here and no signs of hidden treasure rooms. Statues of warriors are
standing around the sarcophagus building a small army.
name: In the Grave

desc:
You find yourself in a cold dank and dusty chamber. The ground underfoot is
uneven and quite hard - closer inspection shows it to be made of solid rock, as
are the walls. However, the uneven shape and unfinished textures suggest that
this place was hewn by no mortal hand.
name: Deep beneath the Grave Vault

desc:
This cramped passageway twists and turns, and in places it seems doubtful
whether you will be able to proceed any further. Progress is hampered not only
by the sharp changes of direction, but also by the small dimensions of the
crawlspace, and by its rough and unfinished nature; sharp outcrops of hard stone
keep tearing at you as you try to move on.
name: In a Cramped Crawlspace

desc:
The walls, floor and ceiling of this chamber appear to be rough-hewn, or perhaps
they were not carved by mortal hand at all, but rather this is a natural cave.
At one time somebody built a stone staircase out of this place, but that has
long since collapsed to a pile of useless rubble, and it seems that to escape
from this place one might need to be quite skilled at climbing.
name: Under the Sarcophagus

desc:
The tunnel is very dark and narrow. You realise that the walls in the rooms
before were all softly glowing, spending light to light the rooms. Skeleton
skulls can be seen in small niches in the wall. You feel the presence of evil
spirits near.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The room doesn't look special as you expected it to. It has the same paintings
and runes on the wall as the room above. Statues are in the corners of the room,
they look like guardian statues. You wonder who or what really guards this
burial mound. A dark tunnel leads west.
name: Inside an Angmar Burial Mound

desc:
Now you have found the place where all this fog comes from. The hill is about
twice as high as an average human and its surface is smooth and black. Strange
red glowing runes are written on the hill. The hill doesn't look natural at all
and you wonder how runes can glow. This hill is surely a magical construction.
name: Strange-looking Hill

desc:
You really must be near now. The fog is so thick here that you can't even see
your own hands. As you go further north-east you bump into rocks right and left
of you. You are going through a narrow passage.
name: Foggy Plain

desc:
The fog becomes thicker and thicker now, but you discover that the fog doesn't
cover a big area. It comes from something in the north-east. The rocky ground is
interrupted by small, dust covered areas. You see strange irregularities in the
dust.
name: Foggy Plain

desc:
This must be an old battlefield. You see frost-covered skeletons everywhere and
you find rusty swords and armour here. There are two types of skeletons, one
kind looks like human skeletons and the other kind is much smaller. Maybe orc-
skeletons? You also discover that the armour is split into three different
types. This looks like a battlefield of old Angmar. Orcs and hillmen fighting
against other humans.
name: Battlefield

desc:
The stench in this hole makes you vomit. Unidentifiable dirt is everywhere here
on the ground. You can't see any furniture. The hole is barely lit with light
that comes from the opening above. You feel a light wind coming out of the
north-west corner.
name: Troll Hole

desc:
The tunnel bends east-west here. Water is dropping from the ceiling and the
ground is covered with a black, slimy mass. Shadows flit over the tunnel walls.
You hear screams coming from the west where the tunnel widens into an inter-
section. There is a hole in the east wall.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
Two tunnels, one from the south and one from the east, are crossing each other
here. They lead west into the main part of this underground area. Skull-headed
staffs stick right and left of you in front of the tunnel walls. This is for
sure no resting place for an exhausted traveller!
name: Tunnel Intersection

desc:
As you enter the hole you see a small path leading north through piles of trash.
The hole was dug out a long time ago and it looks like the entrance to a larger
underground construction. Maybe it was built to give sun-shy creatures shelter
in the day?
name: Troll Hole

desc:
You are in a large room with a big fire in the centre. On top of the fire is a
huge cooking pot. The pot is even big enough to put complete human beings in it!
You see a screaming head looking over the edge of the cooking pot waving at you
and begging for mercy and help. All kinds of eaten up body parts are scattered
over the ground.
name: Main Cave

desc:
In this room there is a large grill. A corpse spit on a roasting spit slowly
cooks over it. From the size of the corpse you can only guess that this poor
creature once was a dwarf or hobbit. In the north-east corner you can see more
corpses ready prepared for the fire. In the small pot next to the grill a sauce
for the meat is boiling. You don't dare to think what the sauce is made of.
name: Grill Cave

desc:
The vegetation is lighter here as in the forest to the west. The bushes are
dried out and there are no trees here. Sand covers the rocky ground but you
can't discover any tracks here. Down you can see a trail coming out of the west
leading towards a ford in the Bruinen.
name: Cliff

desc:
The forest looks rotten here and the trees are decaying. Trees are split in half
and others look like someone with really big teeth was chewing on them. The
undergrowth is trampled into the ground and no signs of living animals can be
found. A cold wind comes from the north.
name: Rotten Forest

desc:
You are in a dark forest. Dead, burned-out trees surround you and the ground
glooms in a dark light. The trees look like they have turned to stone a long
time ago and they have no leaves. You are not sure if they are alive or not! A
couple of trees are broken off and you wonder who and what for they are used
now.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
A dense fog is coming from the north and you can barely see your own hands. You
can't see anything of the surrounding landscape and you are not sure where the
cliff ends. It is very quiet here and the only living thing you can find is
lichen on the rocks.
name: Cliff

desc:
The cliff is a break in the slowly rising landscape. Huge rocks are lying on the
ground making it almost impossible for you to move on, but you have a perfect
view over the surrounding landscape. Down the cliff you can see a trail bending
from the east to the west. A path is leading north-east from the trail and you
can see the Bruinen in the south.
name: Cliff

desc:
The solid, coarse-grained granite has been excavated to create a sort of
crossroads deep underground. The excavation must have required great effort; the
very core of the hard, unforgiving bedrock has given way in the face of brute
strength and hard-headed will. White marks are faintly visible on the dark stone
surfaces, left over from the scraping of metal against stone. Tunnels diverge in
five directions, and the rugged walls quickly fade from sight around corners and
down slopes.
name: Hollowed-out Core

desc:
This tunnel runs horizontally as it cuts through the rock from north to south.
The stone underfoot is rough but level, and a layer of dirt has accumulated on
the ground near the tunnel walls. A series of small cracks in the walls seems to
be the source of the dirt; it is being squeezed through the cracks slowly by the
pressure from above, and is piling up along the edges of the tunnel. The
resulting smooth transition from walls to ground gives this narrow passage a
slightly rounded appearance.
name: Narrow Drift

desc:
A tunnel leads here from the west, through the solid grey granite bedrock. The
tunnel tapers to nothing; perhaps the effort required to dig through the hard
rock became too great, and the attempt was abandoned. The walls are laced with
cracks and the gouges from pick axes and other instruments used in the digging;
clearly, not even trolls could dig through this rock without tools.
name: Dead-end Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel slopes sharply here, coming from the north and leading upwards; it
forms a wide, steep ramp. The surface underfoot is littered with small, sharp
chunks of rock that roll and slide underfoot, hindering passage up the slope.
Near the top of the ramp these rocks become more numerous and much larger,
forming a layer of rubble that shifts when walked upon. The ceiling overhead
slopes at less of an angle than the floor, creating a more restricted tunnel
near the top of the ramp, and a more spacious one at the bottom.
name: Steep Ramp

desc:
The walls and ceiling of this vault are made up of a of rough white stone, and
upon further inspection it appears to be naturally occurring marble. Scattered
bones and heaps of filth litter the ground, and the white of the stone walls is
in places marred by dark streaks that appear to be smeared faeces. The walls are
even and the vault is nearly square in shape, constructed with a regularity that
seems at odds with its natural origins. A low opening through the eastern wall
is similarly ambiguous; it is unusually regular in shape, yet bears no marks
characteristic to purposeful excavation. A wide crack in the northwestern corner
of the ceiling appears to lead into some sort of tunnel, but it is too far above
to reach.
name: Marble Vault

desc:
Rough white stone forms the south and east walls of this tunnel. Looking closer
one can see that it appears to be naturally-occurring marble. The tunnel curves
away from these rock faces, turning from west to north as it burrows through
softer stone and soil. To the west the marble rock face cuts across the tunnel,
and a low, evenly-shaped opening in the stone enters what appears to be a marble
cavern.
name: Marble Wall

desc:
This wide, natural cavern seems to be a dumping spot for rubble and rock. Rock
fragments and gravel of various sizes have been piled in the middle of the
cavern, while larger boulders have been pushed against the walls. Some of the
rock debris appears to have been tamped down to form indistinct pathways.
Footing is easier along these paths, as the stones underfoot are less likely to
shift and slide. To the south the granite walls give way to a whiter stone.
name: Rubble-strewn Grotto

desc:
Tall piles of rubble rise from the cave floor. These piles primarily consist of
chunks of granite, likely broken loose during the excavation of the nearby
tunnels. Between the piles, refuse and bones litter the ground, and the odour of
putrefaction hangs heavily in the air. There is a wide, jagged crack in the
ceiling above; another cavern is barely discernible above, yet despite the
height of the rubble piles, the opening is beyond the reach of even the tallest
troll. The wide, rubble-strewn cavern continues to the south and west.
name: Rubble Pile

desc:
Large piles of broken rocks are heaped against the western and northern walls in
this cavern. The rest of the floor of the cavern, while not free from rubble or
debris, is at least relatively level. The layer of broken rock underfoot has
been packed down, and the rocks shift only slightly when trod upon. A small
tunnel cuts through the western cavern wall, between the heaps of rubble. A
number of broken bones and patches of stringy, matted hair or fur have been left
next to the opening.
name: Rubble-strewn Grotto

desc:
The dark surface of an underground lake can be seen in the northwestern corner
of this cave. When still, the surface of the lake reflects the cavern ceiling
above; this image creates the illusion of a basin of great depth, filled with
crystal-clear water. A tunnel, excavated through the hard rock, leaves this
cavern to the east. Westwards it climbs up a steep, jagged slope. Several large,
cracked blocks of rock rest at the foot of the slope; this accumulation of
rubble, paired with the relative scarcity of loose rocks on the slope, suggests
that the slope has been cleared in a purposeful manner.
name: Brink of a Basin

desc:
The basin is narrow here, barely twelve feet from wall to wall in the widest
part. If it did not continue to the north, this would be little more than a
water-filled hole. The walls look black beneath the water, but on closer
examination appear to be made from a grey, rough-grained granite. The bottom is
uneven, and several loose rocks rest upon it; perhaps they have fallen from
above.
name: Bottom of a Basin

desc:
This is the central square of the village. Wagon tracks lead to the east, and to
the north stands a large wooden building. Numerous footprints lead in all
directions, and wooden planks have been laid upon the ground, presumably to
afford easier passage in an area obviously plagued often by mud.
name: Inside the Village

desc:
Climbing up a ladder you enter the top of the watch-tower. You have an excellent
view over the surrounding landscape. The village is surrounded by barren plains
and to the north you can see a cliff. The tower has a wooden roof and no
furniture at all.
name: Watchtower

desc:
The stables have separate boxes for the horses but aren't used much. You see
harness piled up in one corner. Primitive looking saddles are here and a horse
and cart is standing near the east wall.
name: Stables

desc:
A narrow tunnel leads into the centre of the hill. The main room is a perfect
circle, and on the walls there are Angmarian runes. In the centre of the circle
is a round stone-trapdoor. It glows a deep blue, and is much too heavy to be
lifted. You spot a hole in the northern wall.
name: Entrance to an Angmar Burial Mound

desc:
There are chests in every corner of the room. You see piles of armour and
weapons. Other treasure is placed carelessly on the floor. The weapons and the
armour don't seem like they are oiled and polished very often.
name: Treasure Cave

desc:
A large amount of grey, broken rock has been strewn across the floor of this
cavern and piled up along the walls. Footing is unstable and no attempts appear
to have been made to keep this area inhabitable; the rubble has simply been
dumped here. The large cavern continues to the north and west; any noise echoes
in the large space, bouncing against walls at least a hundred yards distant.
name: Rubble-strewn Grotto

desc:
This dirty little burrow is barely large enough to be called a proper cave. It's
been dug into musky, dark brown earth, with a couple of large boulders or the
bedrock itself jutting out from the walls. The floor is soft and slightly wet
earth with a fair number of large barefoot prints in it.
name: Grimy Little Burrow

desc:
As you enter the room your mistake begins to become apparent. The ground beneath
your feet feels decidedly unstable, and it would appear that this is the reason
that this particular grave vault remains unguarded in any way. The only thing
left to wonder at is just how much of the floor remains to shore up the tomb
itself.
name: Grave
